config.rationale: Next run should be the first clean vLLM baseline, not an aggressively tuned point. We only have SGLang data so far, and the latest speculative SGLang attempt failed to become healthy; for throughput optimization, the highest-value next measurement is a stable cross-engine baseline that isolates the engine effect before adding vLLM-specific optimizations like chunked prefill, higher gpu_memory_utilization, or fp8. I matched the proven-safe context target of 65536 instead of jumping to 262144, following the binary-search rule and avoiding unnecessary KV-cache reservation that could hide vLLM’s batching behavior. tp=1/pp=1/dp=1 is forced by a single GPU. I kept baseline defaults per the rules: no quantization, no speculative decoding, kv_cache_dtype=auto, scheduling_policy=fcfs, enforce_eager=false, and no extra CLI flags. gpu_memory_utilization=0.9 is the standard safe vLLM starting point on an 80GB H100. Estimated MTP/NEXTN uplift for the user’s SGLang command at context-length 262144 is lower than at 65536 because the very large KV reservation reduces batching headroom: roughly +8% to +20% throughput versus non-speculative SGLang at the same 262144 context if it is healthy, with little TTFT benefit and some risk of no gain or regression on shorter/prefill-heavy mixes; given the prior 900s health failure on a smaller-context speculative run, operational risk is currently non-trivial. Estimated vLLM gain from --language-model-only on this VLM checkpoint is usually modest: about +0% to +8% throughput at fixed concurrency, mainly from freeing VRAM by skipping vision components; in the best case it can reach around +10% to +15% only if the saved memory allows meaningfully more KV cache, higher safe max_model_len, or higher admitted concurrency. After this baseline succeeds, the next throughput-oriented vLLM steps should likely be: enable chunked prefill plus prefix caching, then raise gpu_memory_utilization toward 0.95, and then test fp8 if stability remains good.

smoke_tests.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)

llm_commentary: This vLLM baseline is the current leader on all three objectives: it delivers the highest throughput so far at 5728.7 while also achieving the lowest TTFT p95 at 26.9 ms. That makes it not just the best throughput point, but also the strongest balanced configuration by a wide margin versus the prior SGLang best (4395.5 throughput, 142.4 ms TTFT). As a clean cross-engine baseline, it materially improves the frontier and gives a strong foundation for targeted vLLM tuning next.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-vllm-7e985caa18df --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d vllm/vllm-openai:latest --model Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tensor-parallel-size 1 --dtype auto --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9 --max-model-len 65536 --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder --reasoning-parser qwen3
